name: Dill Pickled Eggs
recipeInstructions:
1. Drain the pickle juice into a large pan.
2. Add cider vinegar, water, cloves, and chopped dill to the pan.
3. Bring the liquid mixture to a full boil over medium-high heat.
4. Reduce the heat to maintain a simmer for 45 minutes to one hour (ensure proper ventilation as it can be fragrant).
5. Place eggs, vegetable slices, and pickle spears into a large glass or hard plastic container (a large ice cream pail works well).
6. Strain the liquid mixture through a pasta colander.
7. Pour the strained liquid over the eggs and vegetables.
8. Let the mixture sit at room temperature for 24-36 hours. The eggs will become very bright yellow with a delightful dill flavor, and the vegetables and pickles will have an exceptional dill taste that can be enjoyed separately or as a garnish.
OPTIONALLY, use the dill-pickled eggs to make deviled eggs by increasing the amount of dry mustard and horseradish for enhanced flavor.
